runs.content:
Rigel Pharmaceuticals trades at a ~2.2x P/E against guided full-year 2026 profitability and 26% YoY product-sales growth, a rare cheap-and-growing small-cap picked up as capital rotated out of a broad semiconductor selloff (-4.5%) into value.

No Portfolio 2 trade today — the volume-spike candidate (Crinetics, on Vertex's $10B all-cash bid) was rejected as a capped merger-arb with no upside, and yesterday's chip-sector gainers (WDC, TER) were themselves caught in today's rout.

Walmart rides the 2026 rotation out of AI-semiconductors into Consumer Staples/Industrials/Energy, reinforced by weak June NFP data pushing rate-cut expectations that favor defensives.

Genuine Parts spiked +12% on ~2.27x average volume after a Bloomberg report of O'Reilly Automotive's ~$10bn cash bid for its NAPA auto-parts division, confirming GPC's own February 2026 breakup review.
